title: sambucus cerulea
intro: Sambucus cerulea, also known as blue elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Sambucus cerulea include reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms, alleviating respiratory problems, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Sambucus cerulea include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to prevent and treat infections, as well as to promote overall well-being.
